content: The existing Self-Refine framework for iterative code improvement can be reengineered to leverage the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives central to LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a new self-improvement pipeline that surfaces unrecognized flaws in initial outputs without external supervision. This hybrid framework would generate diverse competing drafts of an output first, then use Self-Refine's iterative feedback loop to resolve inconsistencies between the drafts, producing higher-quality outputs than either approach alone.
